title: Intestinal Obstruction
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Intestinal obstruction occurs when food or stool cannot move through the intestines.
Obstruction symptoms can include severe abdominal pain or cramping, vomiting, bloating, abdominal swelling, inability to pass gas, and constipation.
Complete intestinal obstruction is a medical emergency and may require surgery.
